title: Emoji Madness Takes Over the World!

title: Olympic Boycott Shakes Montreal Games
story: Controversy rocked the Olympic world in 1976. As athletes gathered for the 21st modern games in Montreal on July 17, a major protest unfolded. Many African nations decided to stay home. They were upset that New Zealand's rugby team had played in South Africa, where unfair apartheid laws still existed. The boycott grew, with over two dozen countries refusing to compete.
activity_question: Why did African nations boycott the 1976 Olympics?
activity_choices: New Zealand's rugby team played in South Africa
The games were held in Montreal
activity_answer: New Zealand's rugby team played in South Africa
follow_up_question: What is apartheid, and why was it a problem?

title: Nelson Mandela's Call to Fight AIDS
story: A hero's voice echoed across the globe. Nelson Mandela, once South Africa's president, urged everyone to join the battle against AIDS. On July 17, 2004, he challenged the world to take action. Mandela knew this disease affected millions. His powerful words reminded us that together, we can make a difference in people's lives.
activity_question: What did Nelson Mandela urge everyone to do?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for world leaders to speak up about health issues?

title: Peace Medal for a Prisoner
story: Locked behind bars, Nelson Mandela received an unexpected honor. The anti-apartheid leader had just begun his life sentence in 1964 when he was awarded the Joliot Curie Gold Medal for Peace on July 17. This prestigious prize recognized Mandela's tireless fight for equality in South Africa, even as he sat in prison.
activity_question: What award did Nelson Mandela receive while in prison?
activity_choices: Joliot Curie Gold Medal for Peace
activity_answer: Joliot Curie Gold Medal for Peace
follow_up_question: Why do you think Mandela was given a peace award while in prison?

title: A Life Spanning Three Centuries
story: Rosa Jackson Lumpkin's incredible journey began in 1876. Born on July 17 in Flint, Georgia, she lived through amazing changes in the world. Rosa witnessed the invention of cars, airplanes, and even space travel! Her remarkable life stretched across parts of three different centuries before she passed away in 1991 at the impressive age of 115.
activity_question: Where was Rosa Jackson Lumpkin born?
follow_up_question: Can you imagine living long enough to see both horse-drawn carriages and modern computers?

title: The Beat of a Jazz Legend
story: A rhythm master was born! Joe Morello, the incredible jazz drummer, entered the world in Springfield, Massachusetts on July 17, 1928. He would go on to make music history as part of the famous Dave Brubeck Quartet, known for their unique time signatures and catchy tunes like 'Blue Rondo à la Turk'.
activity_question: What famous jazz group was Joe Morello a part of?
bonus_fact: Joe Morello was known for his ability to play in unusual time signatures, making complex rhythms sound easy!

title: A Magical Illustrator's Journey Begins
story: Pencils and paintbrushes danced with joy as Warwick Hutton entered the world in 1939. Born in England on July 17, this talented artist would grow up to create enchanting illustrations for children's books. Hutton's magical artwork brought stories to life, captivating young readers with colorful scenes and charming characters.
activity_question: What did Warwick Hutton create?
follow_up_question: What tools might an illustrator like Hutton use to create their artwork?

title: Connie Hawkins: From Harlem to NBA Stardom
story: Soaring through the air, Connie Hawkins amazed basketball fans with his incredible dunks. Born in Brooklyn on July 17, 1942, Hawkins became a legend on the court. He dazzled crowds with the Harlem Globetrotters before joining the NBA, where he played for teams like the Phoenix Suns and L.A. Lakers. In 1968, Hawkins was named the ABA's Most Valuable Player, cementing his place in basketball history.
activity_question: Which team did Connie Hawkins play for before joining the NBA?
follow_up_question: What special skills made Connie Hawkins famous on the basketball court?

title: Swimming Star's Splash into History
story: A future swimming champion took her first breath on July 17, 1948. Cathy Ferguson was born in Stockton, California, destined for aquatic greatness. Her powerful backstroke would propel her to Olympic glory. In 1964, Ferguson's hard work paid off when she won gold in the 100-meter backstroke, making waves in the swimming world.
activity_question: In which swimming event did Cathy Ferguson win Olympic gold?
follow_up_question: What other Olympic events do you think swimmers compete in?

title: A Voice Like Snow: Phoebe's Musical Journey
story: A musical star was born! On July 17, 1950, Phoebe Snow entered the world in New York City. This talented singer-songwriter would later captivate audiences with her soulful voice and poetic lyrics. Snow's hit song 'Poetry Man' showcased her unique style, blending folk, jazz, and blues into a sound all her own.
activity_question: What hit song did Phoebe Snow create?
follow_up_question: What three music styles did Phoebe Snow blend in her unique sound?

title: Sci-Fi Mastermind Born
story: A creative spark ignited when J. Michael Straczynski was born in 1954. This future sci-fi genius would go on to create the epic space saga Babylon 5. Straczynski's imagination knew no bounds as he crafted thrilling stories for TV, comics, and movies. His journey from Paterson, New Jersey to Hollywood stardom began on July 17, shaping the worlds of entertainment we enjoy today.
activity_question: What famous space saga did J. Michael Straczynski create?
follow_up_question: Can you name another type of media Straczynski worked in besides TV?

title: A Voice Born for Disney Magic
story: Regina Belle's powerful voice first filled the air on July 17, 1963. Born in Engelwood, New Jersey, she grew up to become a talented singer-songwriter. Belle's career soared when she teamed up with Peabo Bryson. Together, they created musical magic with 'A Whole New World', the beloved song from Disney's Aladdin. This duet earned them a prestigious Grammy Award.
activity_question: What famous Disney song did Regina Belle perform with Peabo Bryson?
follow_up_question: Can you name the Disney movie that features 'A Whole New World'?
